name: Hotel Midtown Richardson
detail: This being my 9th trip to Taiwan, I have stayed at a number of hotels from 5 star to 3 star. I would rank this as one of the best hotels I have stayed at in Taipei. I had orgininally booked for 3 days but ended up staying a total of 6 days. The location in excellent with the Ximen MRT station exit 5 just outside the hotel. The rooms are very modern and the bed is very comfortable. This is a new hotel and there are still some renovaitons in the lower level but nothing to be a hinderence at all to your stay. I have only some very small comments to make but once again nothing to be overly concerned by. The lip on the shower floor is quite low so the water does not drain away as quick so need to be careful of the water flowing over to the floor. While ther rooms are very spacious there is no where to put your luggage except on the floor maybe the room could have a stand that you can put your luggage on. The breakfast is delivered to your room from an external supplier. Not really to my liking as it was cold. I would have preffered maybe some pastries than the american style that was delivered.. My final comment and this is not a critisicm of this hotel but of most in taiwan. I would like to see an iron and ironing board in the rooms. Overall I must say I was very impressed with this hotel and the staff. I will definately be booking this hotel again on my next trip.

name: Caesar Park Hotel Banqiao
detail: Facilities were up to date, rooms were clean and spacious.

name: Tamsui Island Heart
detail: I was pleasantly surprised with my spacious and clean room.

name: Miramar Garden Hotel
detail: Though 5 star hotel, it is reasonable price, but staff are worthy of 5 stars, very courteous and kind. Rooms are clean, relaxing with high ceiling, dignified corridor. Breakfast was delicious while we could enjoy beautiful Asian garden. Especially I like breads and pan cakes in this hotel. This time, we experienced flight cancel due to typhoon and had been in troubled for contacting with airline company, and the staff was willingly trying to understand what I wanted to say from my poor English , though the reception counter was busy, she made every effort to support me, which was very impressive and I really appreciate her. It is a little from from Taipei station, but it is no inconvenient if taking a taxi ( taxi fare is low in Taiwan). Rather, a little far from busy street, we were able to stay comfortably, calmly. I think this hotel is reliable and good enough to stay repeatedly.

name: TWIN STAR INN
detail: It’s hard to beat this level of accessibility in Taipei.
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: We Come Hostel
detail: Stayed here for two nights, supposedly just for a night. The staff was really helpful to arrange me one bed for two night since my booking for two nights is two different separate booking. The hostel is just a few minutes walk from the Airport MRT. So if you are coming to other parts of Taiwan via HSR or TRA, just look for the Airport MRT and exit the Y18. From there, just go straight and turn left after the police station. To be honest, I thought that the hostel is nearer to Taipei Main Station, but it's actually nearer Beimen Station. From the hostel also, you can walk yourself going to Ximending. Their door closes at 11PM, so be sure to arrive hostel before that. Also, the place is a refurbished office space, I think. Their lobby is at 2nd floor. Breakfast also was great with coffee, bread and jams, and sometimes nuggets and fries. I must say their bed is really comfy as compared to other dorm beds I have slept in, so five star for that.

name: Cosmos Hotel Taipei Main Station
detail: The Cosmos probably has the best location of any hotel in Taipei, adjacent to Taipei Main Station. The staff was very friendly and welcoming, with check-in and check-out very quick and painless. The triple family room was super clean, with the best beds ever. The room was made up promptly each morning. The room recommendation I would make is to have electrical outlets nearer to the beds so one could charge devices while using them in the beds. The breakfast buffet was adequate, but not spectacular. Overall I would highly recommend the Cosmos, and will surely stay there again when in Taipei.
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: Caesar Park Hotel
detail: Hotel's location is probably one of the best in Taipei being situated above Taipei Main Station MRT and having its own dedicated exit (M6). Hotel is at the more expensive end of the range of Taipei hotels but I felt I achieved decent value for money all considering. Room for a couple was perfectly adequate, though did not appear as large as some photos I had seen in advertising. However this might have been due to the enormous bathroom taking a fair amount of the room space. I would neither describe the room as dated or modern, somewhere in between, but comfortable and always clean. Air conditioning was very good and quiet in operation. Breakfast was buffet style and comprehensive; I was very happy with it, the fruit juices were some of the best I have tasted. Staff need a special mention as they were consistently delightful, polite, friendly and always helpful (they could speak English though my partner can speak Chinese). Overall a very pleasant 8 day stay and I would visit again.
area: Taipei Main Station,Taipei - View on map

name: Beauty Hotels Roumei Boutique
detail: The Beauty Roumei is a very modern boutique type hotel. It is not crowded, but the lobby is attractive and clean. The front desk staff is very friendly, they always stand when you walk in the hotel. They were quite helpful in answering questions, although they didn't know the area all that well, we had to explore on our own. The public areas include a business center with 2 free computers...never a problem to get on them..a workout room with treadmill, eliptical and bike, and the buffet breakfast area. The buffet was quite nice, same thing every day, eggs, bacon (not too well cooked) cereal, rice, soup, simple dumplings, a variety of asian veggies, fruit, salad and bread/rolls. The room is very nice, the bed a bit hard, 42 inch flat screen tv, with lots of channels, including some adult channels, be careful with kids. All the mini bar is free, green tea, plum tea, sports drinks and Plenty of water! Crackers and chips. We loved the bathroom, jacuzzi tub was nice, with a TV in the bathroom. The shower took a little time to figure out, but well worth the effort, three types of shower. Like most top hotels in Taipei, the heated toilet seat and auto washer is quite nice. Aside from being a good long walk from the MRT, we loved this hotel, and will definitely go back again. The cost of a taxi in Taipei is very cheap and the front desk will have a nice clean taxi at the front door in 5 minutes or less. There are plenty of good restaurants in the immediate area, just 5 minutes walk..go explore if you don't want to go out on the town.

name: Hotel Cloud-ZhongShan
detail: We were very happy with our stay here and would do so again.
